description: The darker part of twilight, when daylight fades toward night. 'Norawhi' names the releasing time—when light lets go, colors deepen, and day yields to darkness in gradual surrender.
sound_symbolism: The word settles toward night. 'No' begins with nasal settling, the day winding down. Then 'ra' recedes, light withdrawing from the sky. Finally 'ti' /ʍi/ whispers farewell, the breathy passage into darkness.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo norawhi' (the dusk). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted as events. Paired with 'horathe' (dawn) as day's threshold times. Associated with release, transition, and evening practice.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Dusk is letting go made visible—the day releasing its hold, teaching us that all things yield in time.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Dusk paints the sky in deepening colors—the transitional palette between day's brightness and night's depth.
